source: Christmas Island Phosphate CO Ltd. royalty account__CO273:666:4
filename: Christmas Island Phosphate CO Ltd. royalty account__CO273:666:4.txt
summary:
- File concerns **royalty and rent accounting** under the lease for **Christmas Island Phosphate Co. Ltd.**, including whether and how to **collect/waive royalty** during wartime disruption.  
- **Geography/timeframe:** Christmas Island and **Straits Settlements/Malaya/Singapore**, with correspondence mainly **1941–1943** (file cover notes extend to 1946).  
- **Key actors:** Christmas Island Phosphate Co. Ltd. (Secretary; Manager on Christmas Island), **Crown Agents for the Colonies**, **Colonial Office** (incl. Mr. Taylor; Col. Stanley; W.B.L. Monson; J.J. Paskin), and auditors **Tribe, Clarke, Painter, Darton & Co.**  
- **1940 royalty settlement:** auditors confirm royalty due **£21,816 16s 10d** (5% of £436,336 16s 8d); provisional payment **£21,819 11s 7d** led to **overpayment £2 14s 9d** to be refunded/adjusted.  
- **1941 royalty account:** company submits an estimated statement due to missing Singapore December accounts; royalty calculated at **5% = £16,702 5s 10d** on net **£334,045 17s 6d**; Crown Agents accept **£16,000 on account** pending audit; auditors’ fee noted as **25 guineas**.  
- **Rent issue during enemy occupation:** annual rent stated as **£3,500**; **£175** rent for **1942** had been paid and was authorized to be **refunded/credited** “without prejudice” while **enemy occupation** persisted (Christmas Island noted as occupied by Japan).  
- **1942 royalty waiver request and decision:** company says it cannot compile full 1942 royalty account (accounts absent from Far East) and asks waiver; details provided: **8,912 tons** rock phosphate shipped to **Australia** in Jan 1942, **sold for £17,155**; additional **1,750 tons** ground phosphate stock in **Singapore** (freight allowance **£875**) presumed unsold/lost after fall of Singapore. Colonial Office/Col. Stanley decide the **1942 royalty claim will not be pressed “at present”**, to be **left over for settlement after reoccupation/with war damage claims**, rather than fully remitted.
